description: Professional engineering career library covering major engineering disciplines.

careers.name: Computer Science Engineer
careers.overview: Computer Science Engineer is a professional engineering career focused on innovation, technology, design, development and problem solving.
careers.whyChoose: Strong demand, excellent salary growth, global opportunities and future-ready skills.
careers.eligibility: 10+2 with Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics from a recognized board.
careers.educationPath: Class 12 PCM → B.Tech/B.E. → Internship → Job/Higher Studies.
careers.workEnvironment: Engineering firms, technology companies, manufacturing industries, research organizations and startups.
careers.futureScope: Excellent due to AI, automation, digital transformation and emerging technologies.
careers.aiImpact: AI will automate repetitive tasks while increasing demand for advanced engineering skills.

careers.name: Information Technology Engineer
careers.overview: Information Technology Engineer is a professional engineering career with opportunities across industry, research and technology sectors.
careers.whyChoose: Information Technology Engineer offers strong growth, technical expertise and long-term career opportunities.
careers.eligibility: 10+2 with Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics (PCM).
careers.educationPath: B.Tech/B.E. in Information Technology Engineering followed by higher studies if desired.
careers.workEnvironment: Industry, manufacturing units, research organizations, consulting firms and government sectors.
careers.futureScope: Strong due to technological advancement, automation and global industrial demand.
careers.aiImpact: AI will automate routine work while increasing demand for advanced engineering expertise.

careers.name: Biomedical Engineer
careers.overview: Biomedical Engineer works in a specialized professional pathway with applied knowledge, problem solving and industry-ready skills.
careers.whyChoose: Biomedical Engineer is useful because it offers focused career growth, practical skill building and opportunities across relevant organizations.
careers.eligibility: Class 12 or graduation requirements vary by institution and specialization.
careers.educationPath: Class 12 -> Relevant degree/diploma/certification -> Internship/projects -> Professional role.
careers.workEnvironment: Companies, institutions, agencies, startups, government bodies and specialist teams.
careers.futureScope: Strong for students who build practical skills, portfolios, internships and updated domain knowledge.
careers.aiImpact: AI will support routine work while judgement, communication and specialist expertise remain important.
cons: Early career may need portfolio or training
